items.title: Feature representations in classical ML
items.description: Tabular ML models treat each data row as a vector in R^n. Concepts like distance, linear decision boundaries, projections and regularization all rely on the underlying vector space structure.

items.title: Embedding spaces in Gen AI
items.description: Text, image and multimodal encoders map inputs into high-dimensional vector spaces where semantic similarity corresponds to geometric closeness. Operations such as nearest-neighbor search, interpolation and clustering operate entirely on vectors and subspaces.

items.title: Dimensionality reduction and subspaces
items.description: Methods like PCA, linear autoencoders and factor analysis search for low-dimensional subspaces that capture most of the variance in high-dimensional data. Understanding span, basis and dimension is key to interpreting these subspaces and their coordinates.

items.title: Linear constraints and modeling
items.description: Many modeling constraints, from conservation laws in physics to budget constraints in optimization, define subspaces or affine subspaces of a larger vector space. Working in the appropriate subspace simplifies both analytical reasoning and numerical solvers.
